keyPoints: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a dimensionality reduction technique used in linear algebra to project high-dimensional data onto a lower-dimensional space while retaining most of the information.
It works by finding the directions of maximum variance in the data and ordering them from largest to smallest.
The projection of the data onto these directions is called the principal components.
The number of principal components retained is determined by the amount of variance explained by each component.
PCA is commonly used in data visualization, image compression, and feature extraction.
It is a powerful tool for understanding the structure of high-dimensional data.
However, it assumes that the data is normally distributed and may not perform well with non-normal data.
